1: package boundary
2: 
3: import (
4: 	"context"
5: 	"encoding/json"
6: 	"testing"
7: 
8: 	"github.com/hashicorp/boundary/api"
9: 	"github.com/hashicorp/boundary/api/accounts"
10: 	"github.com/hashicorp/boundary/testing/internal/e2e"
11: 	"github.com/hashicorp/go-secure-stdlib/base62"
12: 	"github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
13: )
14: 
15: // CreateNewAccountApi creates a new account using the Go api.
16: // Returns the id of the new account as well as the password that was generated
17: func CreateNewAccountApi(t testing.TB, ctx context.Context, client *api.Client, loginName string) (accountId string, password string) {
18: 	c, err := loadConfig()
19: 	require.NoError(t, err)
20: 
21: 	aClient := accounts.NewClient(client)
22: 	password, err = base62.Random(16)
23: 	require.NoError(t, err)
24: 	newAccountResult, err := aClient.Create(ctx, c.AuthMethodId,
25: 		accounts.WithPasswordAccountLoginName(loginName),
26: 		accounts.WithPasswordAccountPassword(password),
27: 	)
28: 	require.NoError(t, err)
29: 
30: 	accountId = newAccountResult.Item.Id
31: 	t.Logf("Create Account: %s", accountId)
32: 	t.Cleanup(func() {
33: 		_, err := aClient.Delete(ctx, accountId)
34: 		require.NoError(t, err)
35: 	})
36: 
37: 	return
38: }
39: 
40: // CreateNewAccountCli creates a new account using the cli.
41: // Returns the id of the new account as well as the password that was generated
42: func CreateNewAccountCli(t testing.TB, loginName string) (string, string) {
43: 	c, err := loadConfig()
44: 	require.NoError(t, err)
45: 
46: 	ctx := context.Background()
47: 	password, err := base62.Random(16)
48: 	require.NoError(t, err)
49: 	output := e2e.RunCommand(ctx, "boundary",
50: 		e2e.WithArgs(
51: 			"accounts", "create", "password",
52: 			"-auth-method-id", c.AuthMethodId,
53: 			"-login-name", loginName,
54: 			"-password", "env://E2E_TEST_ACCOUNT_PASSWORD",
55: 			"-name", "e2e Account "+loginName,
56: 			"-description", "e2e Account",
57: 			"-format", "json",
58: 		),
59: 		e2e.WithEnv("E2E_TEST_ACCOUNT_PASSWORD", password),
60: 	)
61: 	require.NoError(t, output.Err, string(output.Stderr))
62: 
63: 	var newAccountResult accounts.AccountCreateResult
64: 	err = json.Unmarshal(output.Stdout, &newAccountResult)
65: 	require.NoError(t, err)
66: 
67: 	newAccountId := newAccountResult.Item.Id
68: 	t.Cleanup(func() {
69: 		AuthenticateAdminCli(t)
70: 		output := e2e.RunCommand(ctx, "boundary",
71: 			e2e.WithArgs("accounts", "delete", "-id", newAccountId),
72: 		)
73: 		require.NoError(t, output.Err, string(output.Stderr))
74: 	})
75: 
76: 	t.Logf("Created Account: %s", newAccountId)
77: 
78: 	return newAccountId, password
79: }

(End of file - total 79 lines)
</content>

package boundary

import (

)

// CreateNewCredentialStoreStaticApi creates a new static credential store using the Go api.
// Returns the id of the new credential store
func CreateNewCredentialStoreStaticApi(t testing.TB, ctx context.Context, client *api.Client, projectId string) string {
	csClient := credentialstores.NewClient(client)
	newCredentialStoreResult, err := csClient.Create(ctx, "static", projectId, credentialstores.WithName("e2e Credential Store"))
	require.NoError(t, err)
	newCredentialStoreId := newCredentialStoreResult.Item.Id

14: // CreateNewOrgApi creates a new organization in boundary using the Go api.
15: // Returns the id of the new org.
16: func CreateNewOrgApi(t testing.TB, ctx context.Context, client *api.Client) string {
17: 	scopeClient := scopes.NewClient(client)
18: 	newOrgResult, err := scopeClient.Create(ctx, "global", scopes.WithName("e2e Org"))
19: 	require.NoError(t, err)
20: 
21: 	newOrgId := newOrgResult.Item.Id
22: 	t.Cleanup(func() {
23: 		_, err := scopeClient.Delete(ctx, newOrgId)
24: 		require.NoError(t, err)
25: 	})
26: 
27: 	t.Logf("Created Org Id: %s", newOrgId)
28: 	return newOrgId
29: }
30: 
31: // CreateNewProjectApi creates a new project in boundary using the Go api. The project will be created
32: // under the provided org id.
33: // Returns the id of the new project.
34: func CreateNewProjectApi(t testing.TB, ctx context.Context, client *api.Client, orgId string) string {
35: 	scopeClient := scopes.NewClient(client)
36: 	newProjResult, err := scopeClient.Create(ctx, orgId, scopes.WithName("e2e Project"))
37: 	require.NoError(t, err)
38: 
39: 	newProjectId := newProjResult.Item.Id
40: 	t.Logf("Created Project Id: %s", newProjectId)
41: 	return newProjectId
42: }
43: 
44: // CreateNewOrgCli creates a new organization in boundary using the cli.

46: func CreateNewOrgCli(t testing.TB) string {
47: 	ctx := context.Background()
48: 	output := e2e.RunCommand(ctx, "boundary",
49: 		e2e.WithArgs(
50: 			"scopes", "create",
51: 			"-name", "e2e Org",
52: 			"-scope-id", "global",
53: 			"-format", "json",
54: 		),
55: 	)
56: 	require.NoError(t, output.Err, string(output.Stderr))
57: 
58: 	var newOrgResult scopes.ScopeCreateResult
59: 	err := json.Unmarshal(output.Stdout, &newOrgResult)
60: 	require.NoError(t, err)
61: 
62: 	newOrgId := newOrgResult.Item.Id
63: 	t.Cleanup(func() {
64: 		AuthenticateAdminCli(t)
65: 		output := e2e.RunCommand(ctx, "boundary",
66: 			e2e.WithArgs("scopes", "delete", "-id", newOrgId),
67: 		)

70: 
71: 	t.Logf("Created Org Id: %s", newOrgId)
72: 	return newOrgId
73: }
74: 
75: // CreateNewProjectCli creates a new project in boundary using the cli. The project will be created
76: // under the provided org id.
77: // Returns the id of the new project.

1: package boundary
2: 
3: import (
4: 	"context"
5: 	"encoding/json"
6: 	"strconv"

8: 
9: 	"github.com/hashicorp/boundary/api"

13: )
14: 
15: // CreateNewTargetApi creates a new target in boundary using the Go api.
16: // Returns the id of the new target.
17: func CreateNewTargetApi(t testing.TB, ctx context.Context, client *api.Client, projectId string, defaultPort string) string {
18: 	tClient := targets.NewClient(client)
19: 	targetPort, err := strconv.ParseInt(defaultPort, 10, 32)
20: 	require.NoError(t, err)
21: 	newTargetResult, err := tClient.Create(ctx, "tcp", projectId,
22: 		targets.WithName("e2e Target"),
23: 		targets.WithTcpTargetDefaultPort(uint32(targetPort)),
24: 	)
25: 	require.NoError(t, err)
26: 	newTargetId := newTargetResult.Item.Id
27: 	t.Logf("Created Target: %s", newTargetId)
28: 
29: 	return newTargetId
30: }
31: 
32: // AddHostSourceToTargetApi adds a host source (host set or host) to a target using the Go api
33: func AddHostSourceToTargetApi(t testing.TB, ctx context.Context, client *api.Client, targetId string, hostSourceId string) {
34: 	tClient := targets.NewClient(client)
35: 	_, err := tClient.AddHostSources(ctx, targetId, 0,
36: 		[]string{hostSourceId},
37: 		targets.WithAutomaticVersioning(true),
38: 	)
39: 	require.NoError(t, err)
40: }
41: 
42: // CreateNewTargetCli creates a new target in boundary using the cli
43: // Returns the id of the new target.
44: func CreateNewTargetCli(t testing.TB, projectId string, defaultPort string) string {
45: 	output := e2e.RunCommand(context.Background(), "boundary",
46: 		e2e.WithArgs(
47: 			"targets", "create", "tcp",
48: 			"-scope-id", projectId,
49: 			"-default-port", defaultPort,
50: 			"-name", "e2e Target",
51: 			"-format", "json",
52: 		),
53: 	)
54: 	require.NoError(t, output.Err, string(output.Stderr))
55: 	var newTargetResult targets.TargetCreateResult
56: 	err := json.Unmarshal(output.Stdout, &newTargetResult)
57: 	require.NoError(t, err)
58: 	newTargetId := newTargetResult.Item.Id
59: 	t.Logf("Created Target: %s", newTargetId)
60: 
61: 	return newTargetId
62: }
63: 
64: // AddHostSourceToTargetCli adds a host source (host set or host) to a target using the cli
65: func AddHostSourceToTargetCli(t testing.TB, targetId string, hostSourceId string) {
66: 	output := e2e.RunCommand(context.Background(), "boundary",
67: 		e2e.WithArgs("targets", "add-host-sources", "-id", targetId, "-host-source", hostSourceId),
68: 	)
69: 	require.NoError(t, output.Err, string(output.Stderr))
70: }

16: // CreateNewHostCatalogApi creates a new host catalog in boundary using the Go api.
17: // Returns the id of the new host catalog.
18: func CreateNewHostCatalogApi(t testing.TB, ctx context.Context, client *api.Client, projectId string) string {
19: 	hcClient := hostcatalogs.NewClient(client)
20: 	newHostCatalogResult, err := hcClient.Create(ctx, "static", projectId, hostcatalogs.WithName("e2e Host Catalog"))
21: 	require.NoError(t, err)
22: 	newHostCatalogId := newHostCatalogResult.Item.Id
23: 	t.Logf("Created Host Catalog: %s", newHostCatalogId)
24: 
25: 	return newHostCatalogId
26: }
27: 
28: // CreateNewHostSetApi creates a new host set in boundary using the Go api.
29: // Returns the id of the new host set.
30: func CreateNewHostSetApi(t testing.TB, ctx context.Context, client *api.Client, hostCatalogId string) string {
31: 	hsClient := hostsets.NewClient(client)
32: 	newHostSetResult, err := hsClient.Create(ctx, hostCatalogId, hostsets.WithName("e2e Host"))
33: 	require.NoError(t, err)
34: 	newHostSetId := newHostSetResult.Item.Id
35: 	t.Logf("Created Host Set: %s", newHostSetId)

3:

5: 	"context"

8: 
9: 	"github.com/hashicorp/boundary/api"
10: 	"github.com/hashicorp/boundary/api/authmethods"
11: 	"github.com/hashicorp/boundary/testing/internal/e2e"
12: 	"github.com/kelseyhightower/envconfig"
13: 	"github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
14: )
15: 
16: type config struct {
17: 	Address            string `envconfig:"BOUNDARY_ADDR" required:"true"`               // e.g. http://127.0.0.1:9200
18: 	AuthMethodId       string `envconfig:"E2E_PASSWORD_AUTH_METHOD_ID" required:"true"` // e.g. ampw_1234567890
19: 	AdminLoginName     string `envconfig:"E2E_PASSWORD_ADMIN_LOGIN_NAME" default:"admin"`
20: 	AdminLoginPassword string `envconfig:"E2E_PASSWORD_ADMIN_PASSWORD" required:"true"`
21: }
22: 
23: func loadConfig() (*config, error) {
24: 	var c config
25: 	err := envconfig.Process("", &c)
26: 	if err != nil {
27: 		return nil, err
28: 	}
29: 
30: 	return &c, err
31: }
32: 
33: // NewApiClient creates a new Api client for the specified Boundary instance and
34: // attempts to authenticate it. Returns the client.
35: func NewApiClient() (*api.Client, error) {
36: 	c, err := loadConfig()
37: 	if err != nil {
38: 		return nil, err
39: 	}
40: 
41: 	client, err := api.NewClient(&api.Config{Addr: c.Address})
42: 	if err != nil {
43: 		return nil, err
44: 	}
45: 
46: 	ctx := context.Background()
47: 	authmethodsClient := authmethods.NewClient(client)
48: 	authenticationResult, err := authmethodsClient.Authenticate(ctx, c.AuthMethodId, "login",
49: 		map[string]interface{}{
50: 			"login_name": c.AdminLoginName,
51: 			"password":   c.AdminLoginPassword,
52: 		},
53: 	)
54: 	if err != nil {
55: 		return nil, err
56: 	}
57: 
58: 	client.SetToken(fmt.Sprint(authenticationResult.Attributes["token"]))
59: 	return client, err
60: }
61: 
62: // AuthenticateAdminCli uses the cli to authenticate the specified Boundary instance as an admin
63: func AuthenticateAdminCli(t testing.TB) {
64: 	c, err := loadConfig()
65: 	require.NoError(t, err)
66: 
67: 	AuthenticateCli(t, c.AdminLoginName, c.AdminLoginPassword)
68: }
69: 
70: // AuthenticateCli uses the cli to authenticate the specified Boundary instance
71: func AuthenticateCli(t testing.TB, loginName string, password string) {
72: 	c, err := loadConfig()
73: 	require.NoError(t, err)
74: 
75: 	output := e2e.RunCommand(context.Background(), "boundary",
76: 		e2e.WithArgs(
77: 			"authenticate", "password",
78: 			"-addr", c.Address,
79: 			"-auth-method-id", c.AuthMethodId,
80: 			"-login-name", loginName,
81: 			"-password", "env://E2E_TEST_BOUNDARY_PASSWORD",
82: 		),
83: 		e2e.WithEnv("E2E_TEST_BOUNDARY_PASSWORD", password),
84: 	)
85: 	require.NoError(t, output.Err, string(output.Stderr))
86: }

(End of file - total 86 lines)
</content>

// Package boundary provides methods for commonly used boundary actions that are used in end-to-end tests.
package boundary

import (
	"context"
	"fmt"
	"testing"

	"github.com/hashicorp/boundary/api"
	"github.com/hashicorp/boundary/api/authmethods"
	"github.com/hashicorp/boundary/testing/internal/e2e"
	"github.com/kelseyhightower/envconfig"
	"github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
)

type config struct {
	Address            string `envconfig:"BOUNDARY_ADDR" required:"true"`               // e.g. http://127.0.0.1:9200
	AuthMethodId       string `envconfig:"E2E_PASSWORD_AUTH_METHOD_ID" required:"true"` // e.g. ampw_1234567890
	AdminLoginName     string `envconfig:"E2E_PASSWORD_ADMIN_LOGIN_NAME" default:"admin"`
	AdminLoginPassword string `envconfig:"E2E_PASSWORD_ADMIN_PASSWORD" required:"true"`

2:

4: 	"context"

14: // CreateNewUserCli creates a new user using the Go api.
15: // Returns the id of the new user
16: func CreateNewUserApi(t testing.TB, ctx context.Context, client *api.Client, scopeId string) string {
17: 	uClient := users.NewClient(client)
18: 	newUserResult, err := uClient.Create(ctx, scopeId)
19: 	require.NoError(t, err)
20: 	newUserId := newUserResult.Item.Id
21: 	t.Logf("Created User: %s", newUserId)
22: 	t.Cleanup(func() {
23: 		_, err := uClient.Delete(ctx, newUserId)
24: 		require.NoError(t, err)
25: 	})
26: 
27: 	return newUserId
28: }

31: // Returns the id of the new user
32: func CreateNewUserCli(t testing.TB, scopeId string) string {
33: 	ctx := context.Background()
34: 	output := e2e.RunCommand(ctx, "boundary",
35: 		e2e.WithArgs(
36: 			"users", "create",
37: 			"-scope-id", scopeId,
38: 			"-name", "e2e User",
39: 			"-description", "e2e User",
40: 			"-format", "json",
41: 		),
42: 	)
43: 	require.NoError(t, output.Err, string(output.Stderr))
44: 
45: 	var newUserResult users.UserCreateResult
46: 	err := json.Unmarshal(output.Stdout, &newUserResult)
47: 	require.NoError(t, err)
48: 
49: 	newUserId := newUserResult.Item.Id
50: 	t.Cleanup(func() {
51: 		AuthenticateAdminCli(t)
52: 		output := e2e.RunCommand(ctx, "boundary",
53: 			e2e.WithArgs("users", "delete", "-id", newUserId),
54: 		)
55: 		require.NoError(t, output.Err, string(output.Stderr))
56: 	})
57: 	t.Logf("Created User: %s", newUserId)
58: 
59: 	return newUserId
60: }
61: 
62: // SetAccountToUserCli sets an account to a the specified user using the cli.
63: func SetAccountToUserCli(t testing.TB, userId string, accountId string) {
64: 	output := e2e.RunCommand(context.Background(), "boundary",
65: 		e2e.WithArgs(
66: 			"users", "set-accounts",
67: 			"-id", userId,
68: 			"-account", accountId,
69: 		),
70: 	)
71: 	require.NoError(t, output.Err, string(output.Stderr))
72: }

part.text:
The callers in tests still need those with `t testing.TB` but that's fine - the test files will keep using them.

Now let me check where the CLI functions are being called from within the same package to ensure we update calls like `AuthenticateAdminCli(t)` and other ones:
